pages.raw_text:
Organized labor may take a hand
in helping the police to quell the
disorders of the Industrial Work
ers of the World. The matter Is
the subject of considerable COM
ment and discussion in labor cir
Giles today. 1t is believed that
some of the delegates to the CEN
trail labor council will move to have
resolutions adopted this evening
opposing the methods now being
exercised by the revolutionists to
beat the city's ordinances against
street speaking.


1t is held by some Of the labor
union men that unless labor unions
step in and try to discourage the /.
w. WIS 1t will not be long before
capital will be using records of
these conditions against them In
fights for better laboring condi-
tions.


The Industrial Workers Of the
World have little use for the differ
ent labor crafts, holding that one
will "scab" on the other and that
no good will ever come to the la
boring class Of people till they are
all organized under one head.
